description_long: This object is a handheld two-way radio, commonly known as a walkie-talkie, featuring a rectangular body with a slightly tapered lower section. The top of the device is equipped with a long, cylindrical antenna and two rotary knobs, likely for power/volume and channel selection. The front face displays a series of horizontal speaker vents, and there are several buttons and a possible push-to-talk switch on the side. The back of the device includes a prominent belt clip, allowing for easy attachment to clothing or gear. The exterior appears to be made of durable black plastic, designed to withstand rough handling in urban environments. This device is typically used for short-range wireless communication by security personnel, event staff, or emergency responders.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, rotary knobs, side buttons, and a rear belt clip, designed for portable wireless communication.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the antenna, rotary knobs, speaker vents, and side buttons.
description_view_1: Front-right view highlighting the antenna, rotary knobs, and the main speaker grille.
description_view_2: Rear-left view displaying the antenna, rotary knobs, and the full belt clip attachment.
description_view_3: Rear-right view focusing on the belt clip and side button details.
